pipelines.product.title: Pipelines
pipelines.product.description: Ingest, transform, and load streaming data into Iceberg or Parquet in R2.
pipelines.create.heading: Create Pipeline
pipelines.create.subheading: Create a pipeline to R2 buckets.
pipelines.create.inputs.name.label: Pipeline name
pipelines.create.inputs.name.description: Example: my-pipeline. Configuration names cannot be changed.
pipelines.create.inputs.name.tooltip: Must be between 1 and 63 characters and can only contain lowercase alphanumeric characters and hypens.
pipelines.list.columns.name: Name
pipelines.sinks.empty.title: Create a sink
pipelines.sinks.empty.description: Sinks are data destinations where your pipelines write data.
pipelines.streams.empty.title: Create a stream
pipelines.streams.empty.description: Streams are data sources that feed into your pipelines.
pipelines.streams.create.title: Create stream
pipelines.streams.create.description: Configure a streaming data source for your pipelines.
pipelines.streams.create.name_descriptio: A unique name for your stream. Use letters, numbers, and underscores.
pipelines.streams.create.worker_binding_: Allow Workers to send data to this stream through a binding.
pipelines.streams.create.validation.name: Stream name is required.
pipelines.table.empty: Get started by creating your first Pipeline.
pipelines.empty.title: Create a pipeline
pipelines.empty.description: Build data pipelines to ingest, transform, and load streaming data. Click Create Pipeline above or use Wrangler CLI to get started.
pipelines.metrics.data_in.tooltip: Total bytes ingested across all pipelines
pipelines.metrics.data_out.tooltip: Total uncompressed bytes written across all pipelines
pipelines.metrics.events_in.tooltip: Total events ingested across all pipelines
pipelines.metrics.events_out.tooltip: Total events written across all pipelines
pipelines.pipeline.metrics.charts.dataIn: Volume of data ingested by pipeline
pipelines.pipeline.metrics.charts.dataOu: Volume of data delivered by pipeline, shown as both compressed and uncompressed bytes
pipelines.pipeline.metrics.charts.events: Total number of events written to sink
pipelines.pipeline.metrics.charts.filesW: Number of files written to sink
pipelines.pipeline.metrics.charts.userEr: Events that failed to deserialize according to the stream schema
pipelines.delete_modal.title: Delete Pipeline?
pipelines.delete_modal.warning: Deleting this Pipeline will permanently remove it.
pipelines.pipeline.sql.heading: Pipeline SQL
pipelines.pipeline.sql.description: The SQL query that defines how data is transformed in this pipeline.
pipelines.pipeline.sql.readonly: This SQL is read-only and cannot be modified after pipeline creation.
pipelines.pipeline.sql.error: Failed to load pipeline SQL. Please try again.
pipelines.pipeline.settings.delete.title: Delete Pipeline
pipelines.pipeline.settings.delete.descr: This will permanently delete the pipeline, including its configuration. This action cannot be undone.
pipelines.pipeline.settings.delete.modal: This will permanently delete the pipeline **%{pipelineName}**. This action cannot be undone.
pipelines.pipeline.settings.delete.error: Failed to delete pipeline. Please try again.
pipelines.pipeline.overview.failureExpla: This pipeline has encountered a terminal failure and cannot be recovered
pipelines.pipeline.overview.title: Pipeline Overview
pipelines.pipeline.overview.description: View connected streams and sinks for your pipeline.
pipelines.sink.settings.general.id.title: Sink ID
pipelines.stream.settings.general.title: General
pipelines.stream.settings.general.delete: This will permanently delete the stream, including its schema and configuration.
pipelines.sink.settings.general.title: General
pipelines.sink.settings.general.delete.t: This will permanently delete the sink, including its configuration.
pipelines.stream.settings.general.delete: This will permanently delete the stream **%{streamName}**. This action cannot be undone.
pipelines.errors.unauthorized: Insufficient permissions. Please contact a Super Administrator to grant you access.
pipelines.errors.unknown: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
pipelines.stream.http_ingest.title: HTTP Ingest
pipelines.stream.http_ingest.description: Configure HTTP endpoint settings for ingesting data into this stream.
pipelines.stream.http_ingest.disabled.co: HTTP ingestion is currently disabled for this stream. Enable it to allow HTTP requests to send data to your stream.
pipelines.stream.http_ingest.cors_allow_: When enabled, all origins are allowed to send cross-origin requests to this stream.
pipelines.stream.http_ingest.authenticat: When enabled, requests must include valid authentication headers to send data to this stream.
pipelines.stream.http_ingest.cors_origin: Specify origins that can send cross-origin requests to this stream.
Please enter valid HTTP or HTTPS URLs
pipelines.stream.workers_ingest.title: Workers Ingest
pipelines.stream.workers_ingest.descript: Workers can always send data to streams through bindings. No additional configuration is required.
pipelines.stream.workers_ingest.content: Copy binding configuration and code to use in your Worker.
pipelines.stream.schema.title: Input Schema
pipelines.stream.schema.description: The schema defines the structure of data accepted by this stream. This schema is read-only.
pipelines.stream.schema.empty.content: No schema is currently defined for this stream. Data will be accepted in any format.
pipelines.sink.destination.title: Destination
pipelines.sink.destination.description: Configuration for where data is stored in R2.
pipelines.sink.batching.title: Batching
pipelines.sink.batching.description: Defines how data is grouped into files based on size or time limits before being written.
pipelines.sink.batching.file_size.toolti: Target file size before creating a new file.
pipelines.sink.batching.interval.tooltip: Amount of time before creating a new file.
pipelines.sink.format.title: Format
pipelines.sink.format.description: Configuration for how data is formatted and compressed.
pipelines.legacy.label: Legacy
pipelines.legacy.tooltip: This pipeline was created with the previous version of the Pipelines API and has limited functionality.
pipelines.pricing: Pipelines is currently only available with the Workers Paid plan. Upgrade your plan to start building data pipelines.
pipelines.stream.settings.delete.modal.c: This will permanently delete the stream **%{streamName}**. This action cannot be undone.
pipelines.stream.settings.delete.error: Failed to delete stream. Please try again.
pipelines.sink.settings.delete.modal.con: This will permanently delete the sink **%{sinkName}**. This action cannot be undone.
pipelines.sink.settings.delete.error: Failed to delete sink. Please try again.
pipelines.create.connect_stream.title: Create a new pipeline
pipelines.create.connect_stream.descript: Configure a complete pipeline including a stream, sink, and SQL transformation.
pipelines.create.inputs.pipeline_name.de: This name will also be used as a prefix for your stream and sink (e.g., analytics_stream and analytics_sink).
pipelines.create.inputs.pipeline_name.er: Must be between 1 and 121 characters and can only contain alphanumeric characters and underscores.
A pipeline with this name already exists.
pipelines.create.http_endpoint.label: Enable HTTP endpoint for sending data

pipelines.create.http_auth.description: When enabled, requests must include a Bearer token with 'Pipelines Edit' permission in the Authorization header. To learn more, refer to
pipelines.create.schema.title: Define Input Schema
pipelines.create.schema.description: Define the structure of events coming from your data stream (Workers or HTTP endpoints).
pipelines.create.schema.unstructured.des: Streams configured with an unstructured schema have a single value column with the JSON value.
pipelines.create.sink.title: Define Sink
pipelines.create.sink.description: Configure storage settings for your pipeline data.

pipelines.create.sink.r2_bucket.descript: Select an R2 bucket where your pipeline will write data

pipelines.create.sink.table_name.descrip: Name of the table in the data catalog

Files close after this duration (minimum: 60 seconds)

pipelines.create.sink.file_path.label: File Path
pipelines.create.sink.file_path.descript: The base prefix in your bucket where data will be written
pipelines.create.sink.time_partition.des: Use strftime format codes (e.g., %Y for year, %m for month)
pipelines.create.credentials.title: Configure Credentials
pipelines.create.credentials.description: Set up service credentials needed for your sink to write to R2.
Set up service credentials needed for your sink to write to Data Catalog.

We'll create a token with write access to "%{bucketName}"
We'll create a token with Data Catalog write permissions

API token with R2 object write and Data Catalog write permissions
pipelines.create.credentials.access_key_: Enter your R2 Access Key ID
The Access Key ID for your R2 credentials
pipelines.create.credentials.secret_acce: Enter your R2 Secret Access Key
The Secret Access Key for your R2 credentials

pipelines.create.definition.description: Define how your pipeline will transform and route data.
pipelines.create.definition.sink_schema_: Sink schema inferred by SQL query

pipelines.create.schema.read_only_descri: The schema fields for this pipeline have been pre-configured.

pipelines.create.success.description: Send your first event to stream:

pipelines.create_v2.source_step.title: Configure stream
pipelines.create_v2.source_step.descript: Name your pipeline, configure how data will be ingested, and define the input schema.
pipelines.create_v2.source_step.pipeline: This name will be used as a prefix for your stream and sink (e.g., %{streamName}, %{sinkName}).
pipelines.create_v2.source_step.http_end: Enable an HTTP endpoint for sending data to this pipeline.
pipelines.create_v2.source_step.authenti: Require a Bearer token for HTTP requests.
pipelines.create_v2.source_step.cors_all: Allow CORS requests from any origin.
pipelines.create_v2.source_step.cors_des: Configure allowed origins for cross-origin requests.
pipelines.create_v2.source_step.cors_ori: Specify the origins that are allowed to make CORS requests. Must start with http:// or https://.
pipelines.create_v2.source_step.cors_inv: Origin must start with http:// or https://
pipelines.create_v2.source_step.locked_m: Source configuration is locked because resources have already been created.
pipelines.create_v2.source_step.schema_h: Schema fields will appear here once your pipeline is defined.
pipelines.create_v2.destination_step.des: Choose where your pipeline data will be stored.
pipelines.create_v2.destination_step.buc: Select an existing R2 bucket or type a name to create one.
pipelines.create_v2.destination_step.cat: R2 Data Catalog will be enabled on bucket "%{bucketName}" as part of the pipeline creation.
pipelines.create_v2.destination_step.loc: Sink configuration is locked because resources have already been created.
pipelines.create_v2.destination_step.tab: Name of the table in the data catalog.
pipelines.create_v2.destination_step.ice: Only letters, numbers, and underscores are allowed.
pipelines.create_v2.format.r2_data_catal: Managed Iceberg tables in R2 Data Catalog
pipelines.create_v2.advanced.roll_size_d: Files close when reaching this size.
pipelines.create_v2.advanced.roll_interv: Interval must be at least %{min} seconds.
pipelines.create_v2.advanced.path_prefix: Base prefix in your bucket where data will be written.
pipelines.create_v2.advanced.time_partit: Strftime pattern for time-based directory partitioning.
pipelines.create_v2.transform_step.title: Configure transforms
pipelines.create_v2.transform_step.descr: Define how data is transformed as it flows through your pipeline.
pipelines.create_v2.transform_step.simpl: Pass all data to the sink as-is without transformations.
pipelines.create_v2.transform_step.custo: Write SQL to transform, filter, or reshape data.
pipelines.create_v2.transform_step.locke: Transform configuration is locked because resources have already been created.
pipelines.create_v2.review_step.title: Review & create
pipelines.create_v2.review_step.descript: Review your pipeline configuration before creating.
pipelines.create_v2.review_step.success_: Your pipeline is ready to receive data.
pipelines.create_v2.review_step.error_de: Something went wrong during creation. You can retry from where it left off.
pipelines.create_v2.review_step.sql_erro: The SQL query failed validation. Edit it and try again.
pipelines.create_v2.review_step.token_in: An API token was created with permissions scoped to your pipeline resources. You can manage tokens in your API Tokens page.
pipelines.create_v2.validation.pipeline_: Must be 1-121 characters, alphanumeric and underscores only.
pipelines.create_v2.schema.title: Input schema
pipelines.create_v2.schema.description: Define the structure of events your pipeline will accept. Use the visual editor to add fields, or paste JSON directly. Events that don't match the schema will be dropped.
pipelines.create_v2.schema.empty_hint: Add fields to define the structure of data your pipeline will accept, or switch to Unstructured mode.
pipelines.create_v2.schema.unstructured_: No schema enforcement. Data will be accepted as raw JSON without validation.
pipelines.create_v2.schema.json_hint: Paste or edit your schema in the Pipelines JSON schema format. See docs for the full specification.
